name: Raging Blades: A Tale of the North
author: Halvidon Maeraed, Bard of Elturel
description:
Halvidon Maeraed's ballad-cycle follows sellswords hired to escort a northern lord's bride — through blizzard, betrayal, and a duel fought on ice thin enough to sing.

Performed more often than read, the printed version restores verses censored in tavern renditions for fear of offending Zhent patrons.

name: Runes Made Easy
author: Scribes of Candlekeep
description:
Runes Made Easy — A treatise on magic as it is actually practiced — wards, reputations, and the price of mispronunciation. Scribes of Candlekeep is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to before 1357 DR.

Complete copies are rare; excerpted versions circulate among mages who insist the missing pages do not matter.

name: Red Book of War
author: Unknown
description:
Red Book of War — Military history and counsel from campaigns fought along the Frontier and the Sword Coast. Library catalogues list no certain author; the work is dated to before 1357 DR.

Veterans annotate the margins with corrections; green officers treat those notes as insults until the first ambush.

name: Rise & Fall of Netheril
author: Unknown
description:
Rise & Fall of Netheril — A chronicle preserved in northern and coastal scriptoria — dates, names, and the sort of detail merchants pretend not to need. Library catalogues list no certain author; the work is dated to before 1372 DR.

Later hands added marginal dates and crossed out names; historians still argue which revisions were propaganda.
